utente -> exchange — type=message; label=click on accept/reject button
exchange -> utente — type=message; label=require confirmation
utente -> exchange — type=message; label=show exchanges list
exchange -> database — type=message; label=get exchanges info from database
database -> exchange — type=message; label=response
exchange -> utente — type=message; label=update exchange status
utente -> exchange — type=message; label=response
flow 1: utente -> exchange -> database -> exchange -> utente -> exchange -> utente
flow 2: exchange -> utente -> exchange -> utente
the diagram models a sequence of interactions between the user, exchange service, and database.
the user moves to the exchange page, shows exchanges, and then either accepts or rejects them.
the exchange service gets information from the database and updates the exchange status.
the user is shown a list of exchanges and can update their status.
the exchange service responds to the user's actions.
validations: show exchanges list -> show exchanges list
click on accept/reject button -> require confirmation
get exchanges info from database -> response
update exchange status -> response

main -> database — type=association; label=response; explanation=response
main -> utente — type=association; label=show items list; explanation=show items list
main -> filter — type=association; label=insert new filter; explanation=insert new filter
main -> filter — type=association; label=show filtered items list; explanation=show filtered items list
flow 1: utente -> main -> database -> utente -> main -> filter -> utente
the diagram models a sequence of interactions between the user, the main flow, and the database.
the main flow gets items from the database and shows them to the user.
the user can insert a new filter, which is then used to show filtered items.
the response from the database is handled by the main flow.
validations: show items list -> show items list (no condition)
insert new filter -> show filtered items list (no condition)

flow 1: utente -> profile -> database -> utente -> profile -> database -> utente
flow 2: none
the diagram models a user's interaction with their profile and database.
the user moves to their personal info, requests new info, and saves it in the database.
the profile gets info from the database and shows it to the user.
the user can show updated info and insert new info.
the database saves new info and responds to requests.
validations: show info -> show info
request new info -> insert new info
save new info in database -> response
